title: SWOT Analysis
subtitle: Personal Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats
introduction: A SWOT analysis helps me reflect on my personal development through CAS. By identifying my strengths and weaknesses, and recognizing opportunities and threats, I can better plan my CAS journey and personal growth.

strengths: Dedication and commitment to tasks I undertake
Ability to work well in teams and collaborate
Open to learning new skills and trying new experiences
weaknesses: Sometimes struggle with time management when balancing multiple activities
Need to work on maintaining consistency in long-term projects
Can be hesitant to take leadership roles
opportunities: CAS provides chances to overcome my fear of public speaking
Develop leadership skills through service projects
Explore new creative outlets and activities
Build meaningful connections with diverse communities
Improve time management through structured CAS planning
threats: Academic workload may compete with CAS time commitments
Risk of over-committing to too many activities
Potential challenges in sustaining long-term projects
Need to balance CAS with personal well-being and rest

reflection: Through regular reflection on my SWOT analysis, I can track my progress in addressing weaknesses, leveraging strengths, seizing opportunities, and mitigating threats. CAS experiences like the Rosa Parks trial have already helped me confront my fear of public speaking, showing that challenges can become opportunities for growth.
